title: A device for profiling and identifying an oligonucleotide
abstract: The present invention relates to a device (100) for profiling and identifying a sample of an oligonucleotide. The device (100) profiles the oligonucleotide based on its electronic properties, wherein the electronic properties refer to electronic parameters of a Schottky diode formed by the oligonucleotide. The device (100) comprises of a first electrode (110) and a second electrode (120) disposed on a substrate (130), and a source measure unit (140).
claims: Claims CLAIM 1. A device (100) for profiling and identifying a sample of an oligonucleotide comprising a first electrode (110) and a second electrode (120) disposed on a substrate (130), characterised in that: a) a source measure unit (140) is connected to the first electrode (110) and the second electrode (120), wherein the source measure unit (140) is configured to supply and measure voltage and current in order to obtain a plurality of electronic properties of a Schottky diode formed by the sample of oligonucleotide deposited over the first (110) and second (120) electrodes.
